title: Shallow structure of the Northern Chilean marine forearc between 19°S–21°S using multichannel seismic reflection and refraction data
journal: Journal of South American Earth Sciences
abstract: The research combines high-resolution bathymetry and seismic data to examine the northern Chilean erosive convergent margin between 19°S and 21°S. The study reveals a regionally developed forearc basin containing previously recognized features, with sediment accumulation reaching approximately 2 km at its deepest point along the middle slope. The investigation proposes that subsidence from subduction erosion mechanisms explains the basin formation process. Four trench-perpendicular marine seismic profiles from the MGL1610 (PICTURES) cruise are analyzed using multichannel seismic reflection and refraction methods.

location.description: Offshore northern Chile between 19°S and 21°S, Iquique region forearc

data.notes: MCS reflection shot data (SEGD format) acquired during the 2016 PICTURES experiment offshore northern Chile (19°–21°S). Four trench-perpendicular profiles analyzed for forearc structure. Raw shot data archived at MGDS dataset 23934.
51 OBS stations (XW network, 2016) deployed during MGL1610 cruise offshore northern Chile. Wide-angle seismic refraction data accessible via IRIS/EarthScope web services.
Raw multibeam swath bathymetry, backscatter, and sidescan data (2,642 files, ~170 GB) acquired with Kongsberg EM122 during the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise offshore northern Chile 19°–21°S.
Raw underway gravimeter data (Bell BGM-3, 48 files, ~167 MB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise. A processed gravity product (~696 MB) is also available via RVData.
Raw magnetometer data (Geometrics G-882, 65 files, ~2.6 GB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise. A processed magnetics product (~2.5 GB) is also available.
Primary navigation (GPS positions) data from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise, C-Nav 3050 system, archived via Rolling Deck to Repository (R2R).
Processed seismic shot time navigation data from the MGL1610 PICTURES cruise, offshore Chile. For use when processing the MCS reflection data.
Raw singlebeam/subbottom profiler data (Knudsen 3260, 50 files, ~1.1 GB) from the 2016 PICTURES (MGL1610) cruise.

processing.summary: Multichannel seismic reflection data from the MGL1610 PICTURES cruise processed to obtain depth sections of the forearc structure. Refraction data were jointly inverted to produce velocity models. Data reveal a forearc basin with ~2 km sediment fill, consistent with long-term subduction erosion subsidence.
workflow: 6. Joint refraction tomography (OBS + MCS first arrivals)
processing.notes: Four trench-perpendicular profiles between 19°S and 21°S. Sediment thickness up to ~2 km in forearc basin.

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF (author manuscript). Title/authors/DOI confirmed correct. Named profiles corrected: Profile 1-4 → MC04, MC06, MC25, MC27. source_volume_cui=6600 (40 airguns, 6600 in³/108L); streamer=8000m/648ch/12.5m spacing; sample_rate=2ms (confirmed); record_length=16s. Software corrected: empty → Seismic Unix (Stockwell 1999) + PROFIT (Koulakov 2009). All MGDS/IRIS data URLs appear correct.
